# Human Brain Connectomics: Structural and Functional Connectivity Database

> Open database of human brain structural connectivity (DTI/tractography) and functional connectivity (fMRI), including brain network maps and interindividual variability.

## Author
Juan Moisés de la Serna Tuya (UNIR)
ORCID: 0000-0002-8401-8018
Email: jmsernatuya@gmail.com

## Description
Comprehensive repository of databases on human brain connectomics covering both structural connectivity (DTI, tractography, white matter tracts) and functional connectivity (fMRI, resting-state networks, task-based). Includes brain network maps, graph-theoretic metrics, and analyses of interindividual variability. Data compiled and organized for neuroscience research and clinical applications.

## Data Types
- Structural connectivity: DTI, tractography, FA maps, MD maps, white matter tracts
- Functional connectivity: resting-state fMRI, ICA components, seed-based correlation
- Network metrics: node degree, clustering coefficient, path length, modularity
- Regions: AAL atlas, Brodmann areas, cortical parcellations

## Dashboard URL
https://juanmoisesd.github.io/human-brain-connectomics-structural-and-functional-connectivity-database/

Serna Tuya, J.M. (2025). Human Brain Connectomics: Structural and Functional Connectivity Database. GitHub. https://github.com/juanmoisesd/human-brain-connectomics-structural-and-functional-connectivity-database

## Keywords
brain connectomics, DTI, tractography, fMRI, functional connectivity, structural connectivity, white matter, brain networks, neuroscience, open data
